items.text: crates/haematite/Cargo.toml exists with blake3, zstd, and beamr dependencies.
crates/haematite/src/lib.rs re-exports the public API types.
LeafNode type holds sorted key-value pairs and computes its BLAKE3 hash from serialised contents.
InternalNode type holds sorted key-childHash pairs and computes its BLAKE3 hash from serialised contents.
Node serialisation is deterministic: the same node contents always produce the same byte sequence.
Node hash is the BLAKE3 hash of the serialised byte sequence — content is identity (P1).
Rolling hash boundary detection splits nodes at content-determined positions with a configurable target size.
Boundary detection is history-independent: the same key set produces the same boundaries regardless of insertion order.
NodeStore trait defines get(hash) -> Option<Node> and put(node) -> Hash.
MemoryStore implements NodeStore as HashMap<Hash, Node> for testing.
DiskStore implements NodeStore with zstd-compressed nodes written to a directory.
DiskStore reads decompress zstd on load; cached nodes are stored decompressed in memory.
get(key) traverses root to leaf and returns Option<Value>.
put(key, value) inserts into the tree, splitting nodes at rolling hash boundaries, and rewrites the path to root.
delete(key) removes from the tree, merging undersized nodes, and rewrites the path to root.
range(from, to) returns an iterator of (key, value) pairs in sorted order.
Tree root hash is deterministic: the same key-value set produces the same root hash (CN5).
Unmodified subtrees retain their existing hash and node references after mutation (P4).
diff(root_a, root_b) walks both trees and yields only entries that differ.
diff skips subtrees whose root hash matches without descending into them.
diff cost is proportional to the number of differing entries, not the total tree size.
WalBuffer accumulates put/delete mutations in memory as a sorted log.
WalBuffer.get(key) returns the buffered value, shadowing any tree value (CN6).
WalBuffer.commit() flushes all buffered mutations to the prolly tree as a single batch and returns the new root hash.
A commit of N buffered writes produces exactly one path-to-root rewrite (CN8).
DurableWal appends mutations to an append-only file before buffering.
WalRecovery replays the durable WAL from a given root hash forward, reconstructing the buffer state.
ShardActor is a beamr process that owns one prolly tree and one WAL buffer.
ShardActor handles get/put/delete/commit/range messages via beamr message passing.
ShardActor is the single writer for its key range — no external mutation (P2).
ShardActor crash does not affect other shard actors (CN7).
ShardActor restarts via WAL replay on crash recovery.
ShardRouter maps keys to shard actors via hash(key) % shard_count.
Shard count is fixed at database creation and immutable thereafter (ADR-004).
Database::create() initialises a new database with a configured shard count.
Database::open() opens an existing database and restarts shard actors from persisted state.
Database exposes get/put/delete/commit/range that route through the shard router.
No configuration has silent defaults — shard count, data directory, and commit policy are all required (P6).
